core.label: Observe work of breathing
core.reason: Document tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, speech limitation, or apparent respiratory distress.
core.diagnostic_purpose: Respiratory severity: tachypnea, increased work of breathing, speech limitation, or worsening oxygen need.
core.diagnostic_target: Respiratory severity: tachypnea, increased work of breathing, speech limitation, or worsening oxygen need.
core.management_implication: Increased work of breathing changes oxygen strategy, chest imaging urgency, respiratory support, ED or ICU escalation, and reassessment frequency.
core.management_change: Increased work of breathing changes oxygen strategy, chest imaging urgency, respiratory support, ED or ICU escalation, and reassessment frequency.
core.options: Normal / Tachypneic / Labored / Accessory muscle use / Unable
core.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
core.lr_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
core.action: Document tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, speech limitation, or apparent respiratory distress.
core.technique: Observe the patient at rest before auscultation; note respiratory effort, accessory muscle use, ability to speak, posture, and whether oxygen support is being used.
core.when_to_use: Perform in every fever workup because pneumonia, respiratory viral illness, shock, and hypoxemic respiratory failure can present with fever.
core.limitations: Work of breathing may be subtle in older adults, immunocompromised patients, neuromuscular disease, obesity, sedation, or early respiratory failure; pair with respiratory rate and oxygenation.

core.label: Auscultate posterior lung fields
core.reason: Listen for focal crackles, wheeze, rhonchi, diminished sounds, or asymmetry suggesting pneumonia or another pulmonary source.
core.diagnostic_purpose: Respiratory fever source: pneumonia, aspiration, bronchospasm, effusion, edema, or asymmetric ventilation.
core.diagnostic_target: Respiratory fever source: pneumonia, aspiration, bronchospasm, effusion, edema, or asymmetric ventilation.
core.management_implication: Abnormal lung sounds change chest imaging review, respiratory testing, oxygen/support, antimicrobial framing, bronchodilator treatment, isolation, and escalation.
core.management_change: Abnormal lung sounds change chest imaging review, respiratory testing, oxygen/support, antimicrobial framing, bronchodilator treatment, isolation, and escalation.
core.options: Clear / Crackles / Wheezes / Rhonchi / Diminished / Asymmetric / Unable
core.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
core.lr_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
core.action: Listen for focal crackles, wheeze, rhonchi, diminished sounds, or asymmetry suggesting pneumonia or another pulmonary source.
core.technique: Auscultate symmetric posterior lung fields from apices to bases when feasible, comparing side to side; include lateral or anterior fields if positioning limits posterior exam.
core.when_to_use: Perform in every fever workup unless clinically unsafe; prioritize when cough, sputum, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, hypoxia, aspiration risk, or no clear source is present.
core.limitations: Normal auscultation does not exclude pneumonia or early lower respiratory infection; body habitus, poor inspiratory effort, ambient noise, positioning, and prior lung disease can reduce reliability.

core.action: Look for rash, cellulitis, abscess, wound, drainage, ulcer, petechiae, purpura, line-site inflammation, bite, or rapidly spreading soft-tissue findings.
core.technique: Inspect exposed skin and any symptomatic, device, wound, foot, pressure, or procedure sites; ask permission before moving clothing or dressings and document areas not examined.
core.when_to_use: Perform in every fever workup because skin, wounds, line sites, bites, ulcers, and soft-tissue infection are common visible sources.
core.limitations: A limited exposed-skin exam can miss sacral, groin, foot, device, wound, or pressure-site sources; document areas deferred and inspect higher-risk sites when history supports it.

core.limitations: Oropharyngeal findings are nonspecific and can miss deep neck, sinus, otologic, or dental sources; airway symptoms, trismus, drooling, or muffled voice need escalation beyond routine inspection.

core.action: Assess strength, regularity, equality, and whether pulses feel thready or bounding in the setting of fever.
core.technique: Palpate both radial pulses at the wrist, comparing strength and regularity; pair with perfusion appearance and blood pressure rather than using pulses alone.
